name: Improve model training via make train + make infer

description: Baseline crashed on MPS at first backward pass because aten::grid_sampler_2d_backward is unsupported on MPS; cleanup then failed by unconditionally loading missing output/models/.../model.pt after no checkpoint was saved.

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline by running full make train + make infer and inspect failures before changing src/dl/train.py.
asi.rollback_reason: Current training path cannot complete on MPS due missing backward op and brittle final-evaluation logic when model.pt was never created.
asi.next_action_hint: Patch src/dl/train.py to enable safe MPS fallback behavior and skip final best-model evaluation when no checkpoint exists, then rerun the full workflow.
asi.root_cause: MPS unsupported grid_sampler_2d_backward during augmentation/backprop plus unconditional final torch.load(model.pt).

description: Enabled MPS fallback and guarded missing-checkpoint final evaluation; training no longer crashes immediately and reached 55 batches, but the full make train run times out because epoch 1 alone projects to ~3.1 hours and 75 epochs are infeasible on this MPS setup.

asi.hypothesis: MPS CPU fallback plus robust final-checkpoint handling would let the pipeline complete or at least advance meaningfully.
asi.rollback_reason: Even with the crash fixed, the default training schedule is far too long for the benchmark environment: ~3.5s/batch after warmup, ~3236 batches/epoch, ~280h ETA.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the MPS-fallback robustness changes, but redesign the training loop in src/dl/train.py to use a much smaller effective schedule on constrained MPS runs (fewer batches/epochs, less frequent eval/visualization, adaptive early stop) so make train can finish and produce a model for make infer.
asi.learned_runtime: Reached batch 55 in ~228s after warmup; fallback warning only once; throughput stabilized around 3.3-4.1s/batch.
asi.root_cause: Baseline config trains an L model for 75 full epochs on 6471 images with batch_size=2, which is incompatible with the available MPS throughput.
